title: SEGW to RAP guided modernization
subtitle: Full VS Code replay based on the migration sample: SEGW model extraction, RAP generation, activation, publish, and UI follow-up.
outcome: A legacy SEGW OData V2 service is turned into a believable RAP/OData V4 migration plan with generated artifacts and validation gates.

start.text: Open the migration sample and replay the full SEGW to RAP path for ZDEMO_MIG_PROJECTS_SRV. Show the legacy contract, the generated RAP objects, the validation gates, and the UI follow-up.

panel.title: Migration sample workspace
panel.body: The replay is grounded in the local sample repository: legacy SEGW classes, seeded demo data, generated RAP artifacts, and the skill run notes are all visible in the VS Code explorer.

features.summary: ARC-1 probes the backend before choosing a migration path and write order.

search-service.summary: ARC-1 finds the SEGW service and generated classes without guessing class names.

items.label: Name gotcha
items.value: MPC class is not derived by trimming _SRV

read-mpc-actions.summary: ARC-1 reads the function import from MPC and maps it to a RAP action candidate.
read-mpc-actions.result: ApproveProject: POST function import, input ProjectId length 10, return entity Project, multiplicity 1.

panel.title: Function import extraction
panel.body: The generated MPC code exposes the business operation. ARC-1 keeps the action but changes the shape from a V2 function import to a bound RAP action.
panel.code:
lo_action = model->create_action( 'ApproveProject' ).
lo_action->set_return_entity_type( 'Project' ).
lo_action->set_http_method( 'POST' ).
lo_parameter = lo_action->create_input_parameter(
  iv_parameter_name = 'ProjectId'
  iv_abap_fieldname = 'PROJECT_ID' ).

read-mpc-associations.summary: ARC-1 extracts the entity graph and navigation properties from generated MPC code.
read-mpc-associations.result: ProjectSet -> Tasks -> TaskSet, TaskSet -> TimeEntries -> TimeEntrySet. Referential constraints ProjectId and TaskId.

panel.title: Legacy OData V2 model
panel.body: The migration starts from the real V2 contract: Project is the root, Task is the child, and TimeEntry is the leaf. Navigation names become RAP compositions and redirected projection associations.

read-dpc-projects.summary: ARC-1 reads DPC_EXT implementation to separate business behavior from legacy plumbing.
read-dpc-projects.result: PROJECTSET_GET_ENTITYSET performs SELECT * FROM zdm_project INTO ls_project ... ENDSELECT and ignores filter, paging, and order parameters.

panel.title: Legacy root read behavior
panel.body: This is the type of implementation a replay should show: there is real technical debt, but the business model is still recoverable.
panel.code:
SELECT * FROM zdm_project INTO ls_project.
  CLEAR ls_entity.
  ls_entity-project_id = ls_project-project_id.
  ls_entity-title = ls_project-title.
  APPEND ls_entity TO et_entityset.
ENDSELECT.

read-dpc-navigation.summary: ARC-1 traces navigation behavior so the RAP composition model preserves the old UI routes.
read-dpc-navigation.result: TaskSet filters ProjectId from it_key_tab after reading ZDM_TASK. TimeEntrySet filters TaskId from it_key_tab while streaming ZDM_TIMEENTRY.

items.label: Implementation issue
items.value: Reads broad tables, filters in ABAP

read-dpc-action.summary: ARC-1 reads the mutation path before proposing RAP behavior logic.
read-dpc-action.result: ApproveProject sets ZDM_PROJECT-STATUS to A, updates AEDAT/AEZET/AENAM, commits directly, then returns the updated project row.

panel.title: Legacy action implementation
panel.body: The RAP replacement needs to keep the visible behavior while removing the direct COMMIT WORK and fitting the managed draft behavior pool.
panel.code:
IF iv_action_name = 'ApproveProject'.
  UPDATE zdm_project
    SET status = 'A'
        aedat  = sy-datum
        aezet  = sy-uzeit
        aenam  = sy-uname
    WHERE project_id = lv_project.
  COMMIT WORK.
ENDIF.

panel.title: Legacy UI5 dependency scan
panel.body: The frontend confirms which V2 shapes must keep working conceptually: the detail page expands Tasks, opens TimeEntries relative to the selected task, and calls the ApproveProject function import.

approval-gate.prompt: ARC-1 has enough service, behavior, and UI context. What should the replay open before writes?

options.label: Open RAP build plan
options.description: Show the generated target object set and dependency order.

options.label: Open run pitfalls
options.description: Show concrete issues from the migration run notes before continuing.

panel.title: Run notes that make the replay believable
panel.body: The migration sample includes accumulated run learnings. These are useful in the replay because they explain why ARC-1 validates names, write order, draft syntax, and service publication state.

items.label: Class discovery
items.value: Use the actual MPC class, not a trimmed service name

items.label: Naming
items.value: Do not mix old ZR_* stubs with final ZI_* / ZC_* objects

items.label: Draft
items.value: Use "%admin" include and remove computed timestamps from persistent mapping

items.label: Projection
items.value: Redirect child compositions and one to-parent association on the leaf

items.label: OData V4 action
items.value: Composite keys include IsActiveEntity and bound actions need If-Match

items.label: SRVB cleanup
items.value: Published bindings need unpublish before delete

panel.title: Generated RAP object plan
panel.body: The plan keeps the existing ZDM_* tables, rebuilds the contract as RAP, and maps the V2 function import to a managed RAP action.

items.label: Behavior
items.value: Managed draft BDEF on ZI_DM_PROJECT plus projection BDEF

assistant-plan.thinking: The trap in this migration is the generated naming: the MPC class isn't just the service name trimmed, and the draft mapping needs the `%admin` include with computed timestamps removed from persistent mapping. I've confirmed the Project → Task → TimeEntry graph and the `ApproveProject` function import, so I can map them to RAP compositions and a bound action. Writing in dependency order is what keeps activation from failing.
assistant-plan.text:
I have enough context from the generated MPC/DPC classes and the legacy UI. Here's the migration plan before any backend write.

## Migration plan

1. **Reuse the existing tables** — keep `ZDM_PROJECT`, `ZDM_TASK`, `ZDM_TIMEENTRY` and the seed data; no data migration.
2. **Interface CDS** — create `ZI_DM_PROJECT` → `ZI_DM_TASK` → `ZI_DM_TIMEENTRY` in dependency order, with compositions matching the V2 navigation.
3. **Projection CDS** — add `ZC_DM_PROJECT` / `ZC_DM_TASK` / `ZC_DM_TIMEENTRY` with redirected associations.
4. **Behavior** — a managed draft BDEF on `ZI_DM_PROJECT`, plus the `approve_project` action that replaces the old function import.
5. **Action logic** — implement `approve_project` in `ZBP_DM_PROJECT` with EML (`READ` / `MODIFY ENTITIES`) instead of direct `UPDATE` + `COMMIT WORK`.
6. **Expose** — DDLX annotations, service definition, service binding, then **activate → publish → read-back validation**.

I'll pause here for your approval before writing anything to the backend.

write-decision.prompt: Plan: reuse ZDM_* tables, generate ZI/ZC CDS, add managed draft behavior and approve_project, publish ZUI_DM_PROJECTS_O4, then validate by reading back the generated objects. After approval, ARC-1 writes the objects in dependency order.

options.label: Replay backend writes
options.description: Show create, activate, publish, and validation steps.

options.label: Skip to RAP graph
options.description: Jump to the generated object graph and UI comparison.

batch-create.summary: ARC-1 creates the interdependent RAP stack in one dependency-aware batch and defers activation.
batch-create.result: Created DDLS ZI_DM_PROJECT, ZI_DM_TASK, ZI_DM_TIMEENTRY, ZC_DM_PROJECT, ZC_DM_TASK, ZC_DM_TIMEENTRY; BDEFs; DDLX metadata extensions; SRVD; SRVB; behavior pool includes.

panel.title: Write sequence
panel.body: The replay shows why a migration demo needs more than a generated class. The useful part is the dependency order across CDS, behavior, metadata, service definition, service binding, and behavior pool includes.

items.label: Step 1
items.value: Create interface CDS roots and children

items.label: Step 2
items.value: Create projection CDS with redirected associations

items.label: Step 3
items.value: Create BDEFs and behavior implementation stubs

items.label: Step 4
items.value: Create metadata extensions and service artifacts

activate-core.summary: ARC-1 activates the stack together so CDS, projection, and behavior dependencies resolve.
activate-core.result: {"status":"ok","activated":17,"warnings":["ED064 fallback was not required in this run"]}

publish.summary: ARC-1 publishes the OData V4 service binding after the underlying RAP objects are active.

diagnose.summary: ARC-1 validates source state and activation state before showing the generated code.
diagnose.result: ZUI_DM_PROJECTS_O4 published, ZI_DM_PROJECT active, ZC_DM_PROJECT active, ZBP_DM_PROJECT active, no inactive drafts in package ZDEMO_MIG_DM.

read-rap-root.summary: ARC-1 re-reads the generated root interface view to show the actual composition model.
read-rap-root.result: ZI_DM_PROJECT is a root view entity over ZDM_PROJECT with composition [0..*] of ZI_DM_TASK as _Tasks and computed timestamps.

panel.title: Generated root interface CDS
panel.code:
define root view entity ZI_DM_PROJECT
  as select from zdm_project
  composition [0..*] of ZI_DM_TASK as _Tasks
{
  key project_id as ProjectId,
  title as Title,
  description as Description,
  status as Status,
  start_date as StartDate,
  end_date as EndDate,
  _Tasks
}

read-rap-bdef.summary: ARC-1 re-reads the behavior definition to show draft, lock, mapping, compositions, and the action.
read-rap-bdef.result: Managed draft BDEF with persistent tables zdm_project/zdm_task/zdm_timeentry, draft tables, _Tasks create, _TimeEntries create, and action approve_project result [1] $self.

panel.title: Generated managed draft behavior

read-action-impl.summary: ARC-1 shows the generated action logic that replaces direct UPDATE and COMMIT WORK.
read-action-impl.result: approve_project uses READ ENTITIES and MODIFY ENTITIES in local mode, updates Status and audit fields, then returns the modified Project entity.

panel.title: Generated RAP action implementation
panel.code:
READ ENTITIES OF zi_dm_project IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY Project
  FIELDS ( ProjectId Status )
  WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
  RESULT DATA(projects).

MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_dm_project IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY Project
  UPDATE FIELDS ( Status Aedat Aezet Aenam )
  WITH VALUE #( FOR project IN projects ( %tky = project-%tky Status = 'A' ) ).

panel.title: RAP replacement graph
panel.body: This is the part the replay should showcase: ARC-1 is not just returning one class. It connects legacy service metadata, DPC behavior, tables, RAP CDS, behavior, service binding, and the UI migration surface.

items.label: Next client step
items.value: Fiori Elements or modern UI5 TypeScript

options.label: Show UI migration
options.description: Compare the old V2 freestyle call with the generated OData V4 client shape.

options.label: Finish backend replay
options.description: End after the published RAP graph.

panel.title: Client follow-up
panel.eyebrow: legacy UI5 -> modern UI5 / Fiori Elements
panel.body: The sample repo contains both a legacy freestyle app and a modern TypeScript app. The replay can branch to either Fiori Elements or modern UI5 after the backend migration.

done.text: That's the full SEGW → RAP sample: discovery from the generated MPC/DPC classes, the preserved **Project → Task → TimeEntry** graph, the V2 function import turned into RAP behavior, the published **OData V4** binding, and the downstream UI impact — all backed by concrete ARC-1 reads and writes.
